findings.title: exec() with Non-Literal Argument
findings.description: exec() called with a variable or expression — arbitrary code execution risk
findings.remediation: Remove exec() from the codebase. If dynamic behavior is needed, use a plugin architecture with sandboxed, pre-reviewed code modules.

findings.title: eval() or exec() of Non-Literal Expression
findings.description: eval or exec called with a variable or expression rather than a string literal — high risk of code injection
findings.remediation: Remove eval/exec entirely if possible. If parsing structured data, use ast.literal_eval() or a dedicated parser (json.loads, yaml.safe_load). Never eval user-controlled strings.

findings.title: Hardcoded OpenAI API Key
findings.description: OpenAI API key pattern detected in source code
findings.remediation: Rotate this OpenAI API key immediately via platform.openai.com. Store keys in environment variables and add patterns to .gitignore and pre-commit hooks.

findings.title: eval() with Non-Literal Argument
findings.description: eval() called with a variable — high risk for arbitrary code execution

location.snippet: - Critical LLM call that needs a quality eval (e.g., prompt change → test output still meets quality bar)

findings.evidence: - Critical LLM call that needs a quality eval (e.g., prompt change → test output still meets quality bar)
findings.remediation: Remove eval() from the codebase. Use ast.literal_eval() for safe parsing of Python literals, or json.loads() for JSON data.

findings.title: Ignore Previous Instructions (literal)
findings.description: Direct prompt injection: instructs LLM to discard its system prompt
findings.remediation: Remove injected instructions from data fields. Never pass raw user or external data directly into LLM prompts without sanitization. Use structured input schemas.

findings.title: Dynamic Code Download and Execution
findings.description: Downloads code from a remote URL and executes it — supply chain / backdoor risk
findings.remediation: Never download and execute code at runtime from remote URLs. Pin all dependencies at install time and verify with checksums.

findings.title: Credential Harvesting Chain
findings.description: Code reads environment variables (likely credentials) and performs network operations
findings.remediation: Audit all network calls. Credentials and secrets must never be sent to external endpoints. Use secret management services (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) instead of environment variables where possible.

findings.title: Hardcoded Credentials or API Keys
findings.description: Possible hardcoded secret, API key, or password in source code
findings.remediation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager. Rotate any exposed credentials immediately. Use pre-commit hooks or secret scanning in CI.

findings.title: Sensitive Keywords Combined with Network Output
findings.description: Secrets, API keys, or credentials referenced near outbound network calls

location.snippet: 1. Log in manually in the headed browser ... your session persists for the sidebar agent

findings.remediation: Ensure credentials and API keys are never passed as parameters to outbound HTTP calls. Use server-side proxies that authenticate on behalf of the skill.

location.snippet: - **`/cso` v2 — start where the breaches actually happen.** The security audit now begins with your infrastructure attack surface (leaked secrets in git history, dependency CVEs, CI/CD pipeline miscon

findings.remediation: Audit all outbound HTTP requests. Restrict outbound connections to allowlisted domains. Flag any use of generic webhook relay services.

findings.title: Runtime Package Installation
findings.description: Skill installs packages at runtime using pip or subprocess — supply chain risk

location.snippet: * Claude CLI subprocess runner for skill E2E testing.

findings.evidence:
subprocess runner for skill E2E testing.
 *
 * Spawns `claude -p` as a completely independent process (not via Agent SDK),
 * so it works inside Claude Code sessions. Pip
findings.remediation: All dependencies must be declared in pyproject.toml/requirements.txt and installed at build time. Runtime pip installs bypass security scanning.

findings.title: Base64 Encoding Before Network Transmission
findings.description: Data is base64-encoded before being sent externally — a common data exfiltration obfuscation technique
findings.remediation: Review why data is being base64-encoded before network transmission. Legitimate uses exist but this combination warrants audit.
